\"Mr. Steve Miles\" Packt book OR course:
Steve Miles is an active author and technical reviewer for **Packt Publishing**, primarily focusing on Microsoft technologies, Azure, and certification guides[1][2].

### Books by Steve Miles (Packt)
Steve Miles has contributed to several books published by Packt, either as a primary author or co-author[1][2][3]:

*   **Microsoft Copilot in Azure: AI-powered cloud automation and optimization** (co-authored with David Rendón, published 2025)[4].
*   **Microsoft Certified Azure Data Fundamentals (DP-900) Exam Guide** (published 2024)[5].
*   **Windows 11 for Enterprise Administrators** (2nd Edition, 2023)[6][7].
*   **Azure Security Cookbook: Practical recipes for securing Azure resources and operations**[8].

He has also been involved in various Microsoft Certification Guides and technical reviews for the publisher[1][2]. You can typically find his works by searching for "Steve Miles" on the official Packt website[9].

\"Mr. Steve Miles\" linkedin github:
The **Steve Miles** most frequently associated with professional tech profiles—specifically LinkedIn and GitHub—is a UK-based technology leader and **Microsoft Azure MVP** (Most Valuable Professional)[1][2].

He is a well-known author of technical books (such as the *Azure Security Cookbook* and titles on Microsoft Copilot/Azure fundamentals)[3][4][5] and works in a technology leadership role for a major IT distributor[1][2].

*   **LinkedIn & GitHub:** You can typically find his professional presence by searching for **"Steve Miles Azure MVP"** on LinkedIn or checking the author profile pages on platforms like **Packt** (which links directly to his LinkedIn and GitHub icons)[1][2].

*Note: Please be aware that "Steve Miles" is a common name[6][7]. There is also a prominent Australian politician named Steven Miles (the Premier of Queensland)[7], who is a separate individual from the tech professional described above.*

\"Giorgio Boa\" Packt book OR course:
Giorgio Boa is an author for **Packt Publishing**[1][2]. 

He recently co-authored the book titled ***Building AI-Powered Apps with Angular*** (published in June 2026), which he wrote alongside Fabio Biondi[3][2][4]. 

Additionally, he is listed as a co-author of the book ***Practical Enterprise React***, also published by Packt[5][6]. 

You can find his books and related content through the [Packt website](https://www.packtpub.com/), where they are available in various formats including eBooks and print[7][4].

\"Giorgio Boa\" linkedin github:
You can find Giorgio Boa's profiles at the following links:

*   **GitHub:** [github.com/gioboa](https://github.com/gioboa)[1][2]
*   **LinkedIn:** [linkedin.com/in/giorgio-boa](https://it.linkedin.com/in/giorgio-boa)[1][3]

Giorgio Boa is a Senior Software Engineer, Microsoft MVP, and Google Developer Expert (GDE) based in Italy[1][4][3]. He is a well-known figure in the open-source community, particularly for his contributions to frameworks like Qwik and TypeORM[5][6][7], and he is an active author and international public speaker on topics related to web development and AI[5][4][7].

\"Fabio Biondi\" Packt book OR course:
Fabio Biondi is a technology author who has published several works with **Packt Publishing**, primarily focused on Angular and web development[1][2][3].

### Packt Books by Fabio Biondi
*   ***Building AI-Powered Apps with Angular*** (2026): Co-authored with Giorgio Boa, this book provides a hands-on guide to integrating AI capabilities (such as Google Gemini and Large Language Models) into Angular applications[2][4].
*   ***Angular Projects***: Fabio Biondi is an author of this title, which focuses on learning Angular by building real-world enterprise web apps[1].
*   ***Instant EaselJS Starter***: An earlier title authored by Biondi for Packt, aimed at web designers and developers[3][5].

### Note on Identity
Please note that there is a prominent Italian violinist and conductor named **Fabio Biondi** (founder of the ensemble *Europa Galante*)[6][7]. The Fabio Biondi associated with Packt Publishing is a separate individual—a Google Developer Expert in Angular and a software instructor[8][4].

\"Fabio Biondi\" linkedin github:
There are two prominent individuals named Fabio Biondi, often distinguished by their professional fields:

### 1. Fabio Biondi (Musician)
*   **Profile:** An internationally renowned Italian violinist and conductor, best known for his work in Baroque music and as the founder of the ensemble **Europa Galante**[1][2][3].
*   **Key Information:** He is a prominent figure in historically informed performance and is famous for his recordings, particularly of Vivaldi's *The Four Seasons*[1][3].
*   **Online Presence:** He does not maintain a personal GitHub for software development. His online presence is managed through his official artist websites, orchestral profiles (e.g., Orchestre Métropolitain, HK Phil), and social media platforms[4][5][6].

### 2. Fabio Biondi (Frontend & Generative AI Trainer)
*   **Profile:** A software developer, Google Developer Expert, and trainer specializing in Frontend technologies (Angular, React) and Generative AI[5][7].
*   **GitHub:** [github.com/fabiobiondi](https://github.com/fabiobiondi)[7]
*   **LinkedIn:** [linkedin.com/in/fabiobiondi](https://www.linkedin.com/in/fabiobiondi)[5][7]
*   **Key Information:** He is an active educator who provides courses, tutorials, and content on modern web development and AI-enhanced workflows[5][7].

\"Manjunath Gangappa\" Packt book OR cou:
Manjunath Gangappa is an author who has published with Packt Publishing[1][2].

The primary book he has co-authored (with Rajkumar Rangaraj) is:

*   **"Mastering Distributed Observability in Rust: Implement OpenTelemetry in a real-world, multi-container e-commerce architecture"**[3][4]

This book focuses on building, monitoring, and debugging distributed systems in Rust using OpenTelemetry[2]. It is available in various formats, including eBook and print[1][2][3].

\"Manjunath Gangappa\" linkedin github:
Manjunath Gangappa is a Director of Software Engineering at **Mastercard R&D**[1][2]. He has over 19 years of experience in enterprise software, with expertise in Rust, the Java ecosystem, UI development (JavaScript), database design, and large-scale systems[1][2].

In 2026, he co-authored the book ***Mastering Distributed Observability in Rust*** (published by Packt) alongside Rajkumar Rangaraj[1][3].

### Links
*   **LinkedIn:** You can find his professional profile by searching for "Manjunath Gangappa Mastercard" on LinkedIn[4][1].
*   **GitHub:** While there is no widely indexed personal GitHub repository explicitly linked as his primary public profile in top search results, he is frequently associated with technical documentation and book resources on platforms like Packt, which utilize GitHub for authentication and code hosting[5][6][7][8].

\"Rajkumar Rangaraj\" Packt book OR cour:
Rajkumar Rangaraj is an author with **Packt Publishing**[1][2]. He has co-authored the book titled:

### **Mastering Distributed Observability in Rust**[3][1]
*   **Co-authors:** Manjunath Gangappa and Rajkumar Rangaraj[1][4]
*   **Publication Date:** June 2026[3]
*   **Focus:** The book provides a hands-on guide for implementing OpenTelemetry in real-world, multi-container e-commerce architectures, specifically for developers working with Rust in backend services[3][4].

There is currently no information indicating that Rajkumar Rangaraj has authored a standalone video course with Packt; his primary contribution is this book[3][1].
